prompt: Give me the whole safety lifecycle for developing a LiDAR perception system according to ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO 8800. Make it detailed and engineering-specific.
Give me the whole safety lifecycle for developing a lane maintaining perception system according to ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO 8800. Include all item functions in HARA.
Create a detailed safety case for an AEB pedestrian detection function using ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO 8800.
Create a complete item safety case for a highway pilot system with lane keeping, ACC, lane change assist, and fallback behavior. Evaluate all functions.
How should HARA evaluate Severity, Exposure, and Controllability for an AEB pedestrian function in an urban crossing scenario?
Perform HARA for an L2 lane keeping system that drifts toward the lane boundary in heavy rain with faded lane markings.
Perform HARA for a perception system that falsely detects a stationary obstacle on a highway and triggers hard braking.
Perform HARA for a sensor fusion disagreement where camera detects a pedestrian but LiDAR confidence is low.
What dataset gaps could make an AI perception model unsafe for detecting pedestrians and cyclists? Relate the answer to ISO 8800 and ISO 21448 (SOTIF).
How should an autonomous-driving team evaluate whether its perception dataset covers the intended ODD for ISO 8800 safety assurance?
Define ISO 8800-style release gates for a new AI perception model release used in AEB and lane keeping.
Explain how field monitoring, drift detection, and near-miss feedback should be handled for an AI perception model under ISO 8800.
Identify SOTIF triggering conditions for a camera-based lane detection function and propose engineering mitigations.
Explain known unsafe scenarios and unknown unsafe scenarios for an autonomous-driving perception system under ISO 21448 (SOTIF).
How should ODD boundaries be defined and validated for an L2 lane keeping and ACC system under ISO 21448 (SOTIF)?
Analyze foreseeable misuse for an L2 driver assistance system and relate it to SOTIF and HMI design.
A robotaxi failed to brake for a pedestrian at night. The pedestrian was partly occluded by a parked vehicle. Analyze using ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO 8800.
An ACC system did not slow down fast enough when a vehicle cut in at highway speed. Analyze possible failure types and improvements.
A lane keeping system crossed the lane boundary during low sun glare. Classify the failure and recommend improvements under ISO 26262, SOTIF, and ISO 8800.
An AI perception model missed a cyclist wearing dark clothing in rain at dusk. Analyze technical root causes and safety improvements.
Compare ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO 8800 for an AI-based autonomous-driving perception system.
Explain the relationship between Markov decision processes and autonomous driving, then relate the safety concerns to ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO 8800.
How should sensor fusion safety be argued across ISO 26262, ISO 21448 (SOTIF), and ISO 8800?
How can NCAP and IIHS assessment evidence support a safety case for ADAS functions, and what are its limitations compared with ISO standards?
Build a verification and validation matrix for a LiDAR perception item covering ISO 26262 malfunction tests, SOTIF performance tests, and ISO 8800 AI tests.
Create a V&V plan for lane maintaining perception including scenario tests, fault injection, AI robustness tests, and production/operation controls.
Create an engineering V&V plan for AEB pedestrian detection with acceptance criteria and evidence artifacts.
Explain production, operation, service, OTA update, rollback, and field monitoring controls for an AI perception item.
For a generic autonomous-driving perception item, decompose the item into functions and show how each function should be screened in HARA, SOTIF, and ISO 8800.
Derive candidate safety goals and functional safety requirements for a LiDAR perception item after HARA.
